heading: Capitalization and inclusion in inventory costs of certain expenses
label: 263A Capitalization and inclusion in inventory costs of certain expenses
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, referred to in subsec. (d)(2)(C)(ii), probably means the date of enactment of title I of , which was approved . Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 101–239“(i) .—In the case of a personal service corporation, this subsection shall apply to any expense of such corporation which directly relates to the activities of the qualified employee-owner in the same manner as if such expense were incurred by such employee-owner.In general“(ii) .—The term ‘qualified employee-owner’ means any individual who is an employee-owner of the personal service corporation and who is a writer, photographer, or artist, but only if substantially all of the stock of such corporation is owned by such individual and members of his family (as defined in section 267(c)(4)).Qualified employee-owner“(iii) .—For purposes of this subparagraph, the term ‘personal service corporation’ means any personal service corporation (as defined in section 269A(b)).”Personal service corporation1988—Subsec. (a)(2). , inserted at end “Any cost which (but for this subsection) could not be taken into account in computing taxable income for any taxable year shall not be treated as a cost described in this paragraph.”Pub. (d)(2)(B)(ii). , substituted “the plants described in subparagraph (A) during the taxable year in which such amounts were paid or incurred” for “such grove, orchard, or vineyard during the 4-taxable year period beginning with the taxable year in which the grove, orchard, or vineyard was lost or damaged”.Pub. Allocation Ratio for Apportioning Storage Costs and Related Handling Costs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title I, § 1008(b)(8)102 Stat. 3438Nov. 10, 1988“The allocation used in the regulations prescribed under section 263A(h)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 for apportioning storage costs and related handling costs shall be determined by dividing the amount of such costs by the beginning inventory balances and the purchases during the year and by multiplying the resulting allocation ratio by inventory amounts determined in accordance with the provisions of the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference of the conference report accompanying H.R. 3838 (H.R. Rept. No. 99–841, Vol. II., 99th Cong., 2d Sess. II–306–307 (1986)).”

Amortization of Past Service Pension Costs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–203, title X, § 10204101 Stat. 1330–394Dec. 22, 1987“(a).—In GeneralFor purposes of sections 263A and 460 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the allocable costs (within the meaning of section 263A(a)(2) or section 460(c) of such Code, whichever is applicable) with respect to any property shall include contributions paid to or under a pension or annuity plan whether or not such contributions represent past service costs.“(b)Effective Date.—“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), subsection (a) shall apply to costs incurred after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1987“(2).—Special rule for inventory propertyIn the case of any property which is inventory in the hands of the taxpayer—“(A).—In generalSubsection (a) shall apply to taxable years beginning after .December 31, 1987“(B).—Change in method of accountingIf the taxpayer is required by this section to change its method of accounting for any taxable year—“(i)such change shall be treated as initiated by the taxpayer,“(ii)such change shall be treated as made with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate, and“(iii)the net amount of adjustments required by section 481 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be taken into account over a period not longer than 4 taxable years.”

Construction, as added by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–97, title I, § 13809Pub. L. 115–123, div. L. 100–647, title I, § 1008(b)(8)102 Stat. 3438Nov. 10, 1988“The allocation used in the regulations prescribed under section 263A(h)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 for apportioning storage costs and related handling costs shall be determined by dividing the amount of such costs by the beginning inventory balances and the purchases during the year and by multiplying the resulting allocation ratio by inventory amounts determined in accordance with the provisions of the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference of the conference report accompanying H.R. 3838 (H.R. Amortization of Past Service Pension Costs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–203, title X, § 10204101 Stat. 1330–394Dec. 22, 1987“(a).—In GeneralFor purposes of sections 263A and 460 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the allocable costs (within the meaning of section 263A(a)(2) or section 460(c) of such Code, whichever is applicable) with respect to any property shall include contributions paid to or under a pension or annuity plan whether or not such contributions represent past service costs.“(b)Effective Date.—“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), subsection (a) shall apply to costs incurred after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1987“(2).—Special rule for inventory propertyIn the case of any property which is inventory in the hands of the taxpayer—“(A).—In generalSubsection (a) shall apply to taxable years beginning after .December 31, 1987“(B).—Change in method of accountingIf the taxpayer is required by this section to change its method of accounting for any taxable year—“(i)such change shall be treated as initiated by the taxpayer,“(ii)such change shall be treated as made with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate, and“(iii)the net amount of adjustments required by section 481 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be taken into account over a period not longer than 4 taxable years.”

children.heading: Nondeductibility of certain direct and indirect costs
children.label: a Nondeductibility of certain direct and indirect costs
A
children.text: in the case of property which is inventory in the hands of the taxpayer, shall be included in inventory costs, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any other property, shall be capitalized.

children.label: A
children.text: the direct costs of such property, and

children.label: B
children.text: such property’s proper share of those indirect costs (including taxes) part or all of which are allocable to such property.

children.heading: Property to which section applies
children.label: b Property to which section applies

children.heading: Property produced by taxpayer
children.label: 1 Property produced by taxpayer
children.text: Real or tangible personal property produced by the taxpayer.

children.heading: Property acquired for resale
children.label: 2 Property acquired for resale
children.text: Real or personal property described in section 1221(a)(1) which is acquired by the taxpayer for resale.

children.heading: Personal use property
children.label: 1 Personal use property
children.text: This section shall not apply to any property produced by the taxpayer for use by the taxpayer other than in a trade or business or an activity conducted for profit.

children.heading: Research and experimental expenditures
children.label: 2 Research and experimental expenditures
children.text: This section shall not apply to any amount allowable as a deduction under section 174 or 174A.

children.heading: Certain development and other costs of oil and gas wells or other mineral property
children.label: 3 Certain development and other costs of oil and gas wells or other mineral property
children.text: This section shall not apply to any cost allowable as a deduction under section 167(h), 179B, 263(c), 263(i), 291(b)(2), 616, or 617.

children.heading: Coordination with long-term contract rules
children.label: 4 Coordination with long-term contract rules
children.text: This section shall not apply to any property produced by the taxpayer pursuant to a long-term contract.

children.label: A
children.text: trees raised, harvested, or grown by the taxpayer other than trees described in clause (ii) of subsection (e)(4)(B) (after application of the last sentence thereof), and

children.label: B
children.text: any real property underlying such trees.

children.heading: Coordination with section 59(e)
children.label: 6 Coordination with section 59(e)
children.text: Paragraphs (2) and (3) shall apply to any amount allowable as a deduction under section 59(e) for qualified expenditures described in subparagraphs (B), (C), (D), and (E) of paragraph (2) thereof.

children.heading: Coordination with section 168(k)(5)
children.label: 7 Coordination with section 168(k)(5)
children.text: This section shall not apply to any amount allowed as a deduction by reason of section 168(k)(5) (relating to special rules for certain plants bearing fruits and nuts).

children.heading: Section not to apply to certain property
children.label: 1 Section not to apply to certain property
i
children.text: Any animal.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any plant which has a preproductive period of 2 years or less.

children.heading: Exception for taxpayers required to use accrual method
children.label: B Exception for taxpayers required to use accrual method
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any corporation, partnership, or tax shelter required to use an accrual method of accounting under section 447 or 448(a)(3).

children.heading: Treatment of certain plants lost by reason of casualty
children.label: 2 Treatment of certain plants lost by reason of casualty

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If plants bearing an edible crop for human consumption were lost or damaged (while in the hands of the taxpayer) by reason of freezing temperatures, disease, drought, pests, or casualty, this section shall not apply to any costs of the taxpayer of replanting plants bearing the same type of crop (whether on the same parcel of land on which such lost or damaged plants were located or any other parcel of land of the same acreage in the United States).

children.heading: Special rule for person with minority interest who materially participates
children.label: B Special rule for person with minority interest who materially participates
i
children.text: the taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) has an equity interest of more than 50 percent in the plants described in subparagraph (A) at all times during the taxable year in which such amounts were paid or incurred, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such other person holds any part of the remaining equity interest and materially participates in the planting, maintenance, cultivation, or development of the plants described in subparagraph (A) during the taxable year in which such amounts were paid or incurred.

children.heading: Special temporary rule for citrus plants lost by reason of casualty
children.label: C Special temporary rule for citrus plants lost by reason of casualty
I
children.text: the taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) has an equity interest of not less than 50 percent in the replanted citrus plants at all times during the taxable year in which such amounts were paid or incurred and such other person holds any part of the remaining equity interest, or

children.label: II
children.text: such other person acquired the entirety of such taxpayer’s equity interest in the land on which the lost or damaged citrus plants were located at the time of such loss or damage, and the replanting is on such land.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: ii Termination
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any cost paid or incurred after the date which is 10 years after the date of the enactment of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

children.heading: Election to have this section not apply
children.label: 3 Election to have this section not apply

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a taxpayer makes an election under this paragraph, this section shall not apply to any plant produced in any farming business carried on by such taxpayer.

children.heading: Certain persons not eligible
children.label: B Certain persons not eligible
children.text: No election may be made under this paragraph by a corporation, partnership, or tax shelter, if such corporation, partnership, or tax shelter is required to use an accrual method of accounting under section 447 or 448(a)(3).

children.heading: Special rule for citrus and almond growers
children.label: C Special rule for citrus and almond growers
children.text: An election under this paragraph shall not apply with respect to any item which is attributable to the planting, cultivation, maintenance, or development of any citrus or almond grove (or part thereof) and which is incurred before the close of the 4th taxable year beginning with the taxable year in which the trees were planted. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the portion of a citrus or almond grove planted in 1 taxable year shall be treated separately from the portion of such grove planted in another taxable year.

children.heading: Election
children.label: D Election
children.text: Unless the Secretary otherwise consents, an election under this paragraph may be made only for the taxpayer’s 1st taxable year which begins after , and during which the taxpayer engages in a farming business. Any such election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.December 31, 1986

children.heading: Definitions and special rules for purposes of subsection (d)
children.label: e Definitions and special rules for purposes of subsection (d)

children.heading: Recapture of expensed amounts on disposition
children.label: 1 Recapture of expensed amounts on disposition
i
children.text: such plant (if not otherwise section 1245 property) shall be treated as section 1245 property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: for purposes of section 1245, the recapture amount shall be treated as a deduction allowed for depreciation with respect to such property.

children.heading: Recapture amount
children.label: B Recapture amount
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “recapture amount” means any amount allowable as a deduction to the taxpayer which, but for an election under subsection (d)(3), would have been capitalized with respect to the plant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the taxpayer (or any related person) makes an election under subsection (d)(3), the provisions of section 168(g)(2) (relating to alternative depreciation) shall apply to all property of the taxpayer used predominantly in the farming business and placed in service in any taxable year during which any such election is in effect.

children.label: i
children.text: the taxpayer and members of the taxpayer’s family,

children.label: ii
children.text: any corporation (including an S corporation) if 50 percent or more (in value) of the stock of such corporation is owned (directly or through the application of section 318) by the taxpayer or members of the taxpayer’s family,

children.label: iii
children.text: a corporation and any other corporation which is a member of the same controlled group described in section 1563(a)(1), and

children.label: iv
children.text: any partnership if 50 percent or more (in value) of the interests in such partnership is owned directly or indirectly by the taxpayer or members of the taxpayer’s family.

children.heading: Members of family
children.label: C Members of family
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “family” means the taxpayer, the spouse of the taxpayer, and any of their children who have not attained age 18 before the close of the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a plant which will have more than 1 crop or yield, the period before the 1st marketable crop or yield from such plant, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any other plant, the period before such plant is reasonably expected to be disposed of.

children.heading: Rule for determining period
children.label: B Rule for determining period
children.text: In the case of a plant grown in commercial quantities in the United States, the preproductive period for such plant if grown in the United States shall be based on the nationwide weighted average preproductive period for such plant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “farming business” means the trade or business of farming.

children.heading: Certain trades and businesses included
children.label: B Certain trades and businesses included
i
children.text: operating a nursery or sod farm, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the raising or harvesting of trees bearing fruit, nuts, or other crops, or ornamental trees.

children.heading: Certain inventory valuation methods permitted
children.label: 5 Certain inventory valuation methods permitted
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulations permit the taxpayer to use reasonable inventory valuation methods to compute the amount required to be capitalized under subsection (a) in the case of any plant.

children.heading: Special rules for allocation of interest to property produced by the taxpayer
children.label: f Special rules for allocation of interest to property produced by the taxpayer

children.heading: Interest capitalized only in certain cases
children.label: 1 Interest capitalized only in certain cases
A
children.text: paid or incurred during the production period, and

children.label: i
children.text: a long useful life,

children.label: ii
children.text: an estimated production period exceeding 2 years, or

children.label: iii
children.text: an estimated production period exceeding 1 year and a cost exceeding $1,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: interest on any indebtedness directly attributable to production expenditures with respect to such property shall be assigned to such property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: interest on any other indebtedness shall be assigned to such property to the extent that the taxpayer’s interest costs could have been reduced if production expenditures (not attributable to indebtedness described in clause (i)) had not been incurred.

children.heading: Exception for qualified residence interest
children.label: B Exception for qualified residence interest
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any qualified residence interest (within the meaning of section 163(h)).

children.heading: Special rule for flow-through entities
children.label: C Special rule for flow-through entities
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, in the case of any flow-through entity, this paragraph shall be applied first at the entity level and then at the beneficiary level.

children.heading: Interest relating to property used to produce property
children.label: 3 Interest relating to property used to produce property
children.text: This subsection shall apply to any interest on indebtedness allocable (as determined under paragraph (2)) to property used to produce property to which this subsection applies to the extent such interest is allocable (as so determined) to the produced property.

children.heading: Exemption for aging process of beer, wine, and distilled spirits
children.label: 4 Exemption for aging process of beer, wine, and distilled spirits
A
children.text: beer (as defined in section 5052(a)),

children.label: B
children.text: wine (as described in section 5041(a)), or

children.label: C
children.text: distilled spirits (as defined in section 5002(a)(8)), except such spirits that are unfit for use for beverage purposes.

children.label: i
children.text: real property, or

children.label: ii
children.text: property with a class life of 20 years or more (as determined under section 168).

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the date on which production of the property begins, and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (4), ending on the date on which the property is ready to be placed in service or is ready to be held for sale.

children.heading: Production expenditures
children.label: C Production expenditures
children.text: The term “production expenditures” means the costs (whether or not incurred during the production period) required to be capitalized under subsection (a) with respect to the property.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “produce” includes construct, build, install, manufacture, develop, or improve.

children.heading: Treatment of property produced under contract for the taxpayer
children.label: 2 Treatment of property produced under contract for the taxpayer
children.text: The taxpayer shall be treated as producing any property produced for the taxpayer under a contract with the taxpayer; except that only costs paid or incurred by the taxpayer (whether under such contract or otherwise) shall be taken into account in applying subsection (a) to the taxpayer.

children.heading: Exemption for free lance authors, photographers, and artists
children.label: h Exemption for free lance authors, photographers, and artists

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this section shall require the capitalization of any qualified creative expense.

children.label: A
children.text: which is paid or incurred by an individual in the trade or business of such individual (other than as an employee) of being a writer, photographer, or artist, and

children.label: B
children.text: which, without regard to this section, would be allowable as a deduction for the taxable year.

children.heading: Writer
children.label: A Writer
children.text: The term “writer” means any individual if the personal efforts of such individual create (or may reasonably be expected to create) a literary manuscript, musical composition (including any accompanying words), or dance score.

children.heading: Photographer
children.label: B Photographer
children.text: The term “photographer” means any individual if the personal efforts of such individual create (or may reasonably be expected to create) a photograph or photographic negative or transparency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “artist” means any individual if the personal efforts of such individual create (or may reasonably be expected to create) a picture, painting, sculpture, statue, etching, drawing, cartoon, graphic design, or original print edition.

children.label: I
children.text: The originality and uniqueness of the item created (or to be created).

children.label: II
children.text: The predominance of aesthetic value over utilitarian value of the item created (or to be created).

children.label: I
children.text: substantially all of the stock of a corporation is owned by a qualified employee-owner and members of his family (as defined in section 267(c)(4)), and

children.label: II
children.text: the principal activity of such corporation is performance of personal services directly related to the activities of the qualified employee-owner and such services are substantially performed by the qualified employee-owner,

children.heading: Qualified employee-owner
children.label: ii Qualified employee-owner
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified employee-owner” means any individual who is an employee-owner of the corporation (as defined in section 269A(b)(2)) and who is a writer, photographer, or artist.

children.heading: Exemption for certain small businesses
children.label: i Exemption for certain small businesses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer (other than a tax shelter prohibited from using the cash receipts and disbursements method of accounting under section 448(a)(3)) which meets the gross receipts test of section 448(c) for any taxable year, this section shall not apply with respect to such taxpayer for such taxable year.

children.heading: Application of gross receipts test to individuals, etc.
children.label: 2 Application of gross receipts test to individuals, etc.
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer which is not a corporation or a partnership, the gross receipts test of section 448(c) shall be applied in the same manner as if each trade or business of such taxpayer were a corporation or partnership.

children.heading: Coordination with section 481
children.label: 3 Coordination with section 481
children.text: Any change in method of accounting made pursuant to this subsection shall be treated for purposes of section 481 as initiated by the taxpayer and made with the consent of the Secretary.
regulations to prevent the use of related parties, pass-thru entities, or intermediaries to avoid the application of this section, and
regulations providing for simplified procedures for the application of this section in the case of property described in subsection (b)(2).
